description: Architectural debt reduction — split app_router, move data layer imports out of screens, wire offline sync to repositories

userStories.title: Split app_router.dart into per-feature route config files
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/shared/core/router/app_router.dart — it is 900+ lines with 70+ routes
Create route config files for each major feature group:
lib/shared/core/router/routes/auth_routes.dart — login, signup, onboarding, splash, paywall
lib/shared/core/router/routes/meal_routes.dart — all meals/* routes
lib/shared/core/router/routes/health_routes.dart — all health/* routes
lib/shared/core/router/routes/workout_routes.dart — workouts, exercise browser, plans, logging
lib/shared/core/router/routes/profile_routes.dart — settings, profile, goals, supplements
lib/shared/core/router/routes/feature_routes.dart — pantry, recipes, shopping, bloodwork, habits, reminders, insights, daily coach
Each file exports a static List<GoRoute> method that returns the routes for that feature
app_router.dart imports all route files and assembles them into the StatefulShellRoute branches
All routes must still work after the split — no broken navigation
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Keep AppRouter class, GoRouterAuthRefresh, providers, and route guards in app_router.dart. Only move the GoRoute definitions to separate files. Each feature file needs access to ref for activeProfileIdProvider — pass it as a parameter to the route builder functions.

userStories.title: Move direct Supabase calls from screens into providers — meal_plan_screen focus
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/meals/presentation/meal_plan_screen.dart — 2000+ lines
Find all Supabase.instance.client references in the file
Find all direct repository instantiations (e.g., CustomMacroTargetRepository(...), ProfileRepository(...))
Replace Supabase.instance.client.auth.currentUser with a provider or ref.read
Replace direct repo instantiations with ref.read of existing providers
If a provider doesn't exist for a repository, check if one exists elsewhere or create one
The screen should only import providers, not repositories directly
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: meal_plan_screen.dart is the worst offender — 5 direct Supabase.instance.client usages, imports MacroCalculator and CustomMacroTargetRepository directly. ProfileRepository is accessed via profileRepositoryProvider. For auth, use Supabase.instance.client.auth.currentUser?.id — this is acceptable as a read-only auth accessor.

userStories.title: Wire offline queue to meal and workout repository writes
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/shared/core/sync/sync_engine.dart for the OfflineQueue interface
Read lib/shared/core/sync/conflict_resolver.dart
Read lib/features/meals/data/meal_repository.dart logMeal method
Read lib/features/workouts/data/workout_repository.dart for save/complete methods
Create a write-behind wrapper: when offline, write to Hive queue instead of failing silently
When connectivity returns, SyncEngine drains the queue and replays writes to Supabase
Meal logging must work offline — write to Hive, return optimistically, sync later
Workout logging must work offline — same pattern
Conflict resolution: last-write-wins for user-entered data (meals, workouts)
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: The SyncEngine infrastructure exists but nothing feeds the OfflineQueue. The simplest approach: add a try/catch in repository write methods — on network error, serialize the operation to Hive OfflineQueue. SyncEngine already has the drain logic. ConnectivityService provides the online/offline stream.
